mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations and lower corporate borrowing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series deviates from its 12-month trend by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market that supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 320 signal entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 320.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms synchronized macroeconomic demand strength, which boosts corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered observations during mid-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal manufacturing contraction and inventory drawdowns, which trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a positive Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window.
